title: -Amend Berkeley Police Association (BPA) MOU Section 19.5 Administrative Leave for Captains (50 hours increased to 60 hours)

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution amending Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) for Berkeley
Police Association (BPA) MOU Section 19.5 Administrative Leave for Captains to increase
administrative leave from 50 to 60 hours.
sections.fiscal_impacts: The fiscal impact for four Captains is approximately $4,422.00 annually.
sections.current_situation:
In the most recent round of labor negotiations with Local One, the City increased the
amount of administrative leave from 50 hours to 60 hours. This increase triggered a meet
and confer requirement outlined in BPA MOU Section 19.5.3 which states, “During the
term of this Agreement, in the event of another represented management level City
employees group receive an increase to Administrative Leave resulting in greater than fifty
(50) hours of Administrative Leave per year, the parties agree to meet and confer
regarding comparable benefits for Police Captains.”
sections.background:
BPA represents Police Officers, Police Sergeants, Police Lieutenants, and Police
Captains. Of these, Police Captain is the only classification designated as FLSA exempt,
meaning employees in this classification do not earn overtime. In lieu of earning overtime,
Police Captains have been credited with 50 hours of Administrative Leave each January.

Amend Berkeley Police Association (BPA) MOU Section 19.5 Administrative Leave for Captains (50 hours
increased to 60 hours)
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There is no environmental impact.
sections.rationale:
Amending the BPA MOU to increase the amount of administrative leave from 50 hours to
60 hours annually fulfills the City’s meet and confer requirement that was triggered by the
increase of similar hours granted to Local One and awards comparable benefits for Police
Captains.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Monica Walker, Interim Director of Human Resources, (510) 981-6818.

Consent Calendar
April 29, 2025
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AMEND BERKELEY POLICE ASSOCIATION (BPA) MOU
SECTION 19.5 ADMINISTRATIVE LEAVE FOR CAPTAINS
WHEREAS, the Berkeley Police Association (BPA) Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Section
19.5 Administrative Leave for Captains currently credits Police Captains with fifty (50) hours of
Administrative Leave in lieu of overtime; and
WHEREAS, the Berkeley Police Association (BPA) Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Section
19.5.3 states, “During the term of this Agreement, in the event of another represented
management level City employees group receive an increase to Administrative Leave resulting in
greater than fifty (50) hours of Administrative Leave per year, the parties agree to meet and confer
regarding comparable benefits for Police Captains;” and
WHEREAS, in the most recent round of labor negotiations with Local One, the City increased the
amount of administrative leave from 50 hours to 60 hours, which triggered a meet and confer
requirement outlined in BPA MOU Section 19.5.3; and
WHEREAS, during the meet and confer process, the City and BPA tentatively agreed to increase
the administrative leave for Police Captains from 50 hours to 60 hours.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that Berkeley Police
Association (BPA) MOU Section 19.5 Administrative Leave for Captains will be amended to
increase administrative leave for Police Captains from 50 hours to 60 hours, credited every
January 1 thereafter during the term of the ag
